notes.anchor: on the forty-seventh day from Rome
notes.note: The interval a private letter took to travel from Rome to Cicero's camp in Cilicia. Cicero notes it as fast for so vast a distance, a glimpse of the strained communications underpinning a frontier command.

notes.anchor: About the Atilian debt I have written to Philotimus not to dun Messalla for it
notes.note: A money matter Cicero managed at long range through Terentia's freedman-agent Philotimus, instructing him not to press M. Valerius Messalla Rufus for repayment of a debt connected with one Atilius.

notes.anchor: About Patro and your fellow-students --- the trouble I took over the ruins at Melita
notes.note: Patro was the scholarch of the Epicurean school at Athens, of which Atticus and his circle were 'fellow-students'. Cicero had intervened on Patro's behalf over the threatened demolition of the ruined house of Epicurus, here recalled as 'the ruins at Melita' (the Athenian deme Melite).
